channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The great Easter basket question
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — Love them or hate them, those marshmallow Peeps that come in blindingly bright colors and an array of flavors are inescapable around the Easter holiday.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Joe Biden will speak about Social Security in his return to national stage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Joe Biden returns to the national stage Tuesday to elevate liberal concerns that President Donald Trump's agenda is threatening the health of Social Security. The 82-year-old Democrat has largely avoided speaking publicly since leaving the White House in January.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Study finds more people are obtaining abortions but fewer are traveling to other states for it
item.description: A new study has found that fewer people crossed state lines for abortions in 2024 than the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Officials work to uncover the motive for fire set at Pennsylvania governor's residence
item.description: Investigators are working to uncover the motive behind an arson fire over the weekend at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion, the latest act of political violence in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo And Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive bluefin tuna catches halted at NC pier after officials step in, manager says
item.description: For several days a huge bluefin tuna were caught at Jennette's Pier in Nags Head, but the bluefin fun came to an end after an official visited last week, the pier said.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WGN 9

item.title: Salvadoran fugitive convicted in deadly attack of Maryland hiker
item.description: BEL AIR, Md. (AP) — A fugitive from El Salvador was convicted Monday in the 2023 slaying of a Maryland woman who was attacked while exercising on a popular hiking trail northeast of Baltimore.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Months after CEO's killing, an intruder is arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters in Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A man was arrested near UnitedHealthcare’s headquarters in Minnesota after threatening violence, months after the company's CEO was killed, authorities said Monday. The man was spotted around 11 a.m.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Phone calls, police interview footage shared during Lori Vallow Daybell murder trial
item.description: PHOENIX (ABC4) -- The Idaho mother convicted of killing her two children returned to the courtroom Monday on charges of conspiracy to murder her husband, Charles Vallow. Lori Vallow Daybell is representing herself in Maricopa County during this trial.
item.dc:creator: Sorina Trauntvein, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Female soldier becomes the first woman to compete in the grueling Army Ranger contest
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A female Army Ranger for the first time competed in the annual Best Ranger Competition, and her two-soldier team finished the grueling three-day event over the weekend and came in 14th overall. First Lt. Gabrielle White, and her teammate, Capt.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeff Bezos takes a tumble while circling Blue Origin capsule carrying fiancée: Watch
item.description: Blue Origin founder Jeff Bezos was seen taking a fall to the ground nearly head first, after the New Shepard space capsule returned to Earth in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California police officers plead guilty to painting swastika on car
item.description: “Vandalizing property with hateful messages is reprehensible and violates the oath police officers take,” District Attorney Nathan Hochman said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake strikes near San Diego
item.description: A powerful 5.2 magnitude earthquake rattled the San Diego County area Monday morning. According to the U.S. Geological Survey, the quake struck at 10:08 a.m. The epicenter was about 2.5 miles South of Julian, at a depth of roughly 8 miles.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake shakes Southern California
item.description: A 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck San Diego County at 10:08 a.m. Monday morning, with its epicenter near Julian.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, NewsNation

item.title: Southern California rocked by a strong earthquake near San Diego
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Southern California was rocked by a strong earthquake Monday morning near San Diego. The earthquake had a preliminary magnitude of 5.2 and was near the mountain town of Julian, east of San Diego, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pennsylvania fire chief credits closed doors for saving lives during governor's mansion attack
item.description: Harrisburg Fire Chief Brian Enterline discussed difficulties accessing the Pennsylvania governor's mansion during the fire and what likely saved lives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Congress to end daylight saving time
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday urged Congress to “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in his latest dig at the semiannual changing of clocks.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4

item.title: Family of American found dead on Bahamas beach fears ‘evidence will disappear,’ pleads for help
item.description: Maryland man Dinari McAlmont's family is pleading for "international help" after the 23-year-old was found dead on a Bahamas beach on April 6 during a family vacation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC helicopter tour company shuts down operations after crash that killed 6
item.description: The helicopter, a Bell 206 L-4, crashed into the Hudson River Thursday, killing all six people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: 2022 NCAA woman of the year, family members killed in upstate New York plane crash
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: Stocks rose worldwide Monday after President Donald Trump relaxed some of his tariffs, for now at least, and as stress from within the U.S. bond market seems to be easing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Suspect arsonist was prepared to attack Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro with hammer, court docs reveal
item.description: Court documents concerning Cody Balmer, the man charged in connection with a fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence, have revealed details of the suspect's alleged plot.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, KRON4

item.title: Wedding travel: How to minimize costs to make it work
item.description: Nearly 1 in 10 Americans are going to a wedding this summer that requires at least one night away from home
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, KRON4

item.title: Judge relaxes ban on DOGE access to sensitive US Treasury information
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge has relaxed a ban she’d put on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency preventing it from accessing sensitive Treasury Department information related to millions of Americans. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kansas man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KRON4

item.title: Pet food recalled after testing positive for salmonella and listeria: FDA
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a voluntary recall of certain puppy and kitten foods.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: A bucket of beans and someone's pet turtle: Uber reveals 'most unique' items passengers left behind
item.description: It's no surprise that phones, wallets and keys are often left behind in rideshare vehicles. But a urinal or a taxidermied rabbit? Those are slightly more rare.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: These bars and restaurants are being called the most 'outstanding' in the country
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Foodies and restaurant lovers across the U.S. will need to add some new spots to their lists of places to eat with the James Beard Foundation officially announcing its finalists for this year’s most “outstanding” restaurant and bar in the country.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KRON4

item.title: Trump undergoes his annual physical after years of reluctance to share medical information
item.description: Donald Trump had his annual physical on Friday, a check-up that may give the public its first details in years about the health of a man who, in January, became the oldest in U.S. history to be sworn in as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he would respect Supreme Court decision to return wrongly deported man
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE — President Trump said Friday he would respect a Supreme Court decision to bring back a Maryland man the administration mistakenly deported to El Salvador. “If the Supreme Court said bring somebody back I would do that.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices across the country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WGN 9
